zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir於0996年出生在法國諾門迪艾盧維爾斯時，他的父親Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches36歲，母親Judith de Bayeux34歲。他和Adèle Osule至少有1個兒子。他去世於1038年在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: St. Brice's Day Massacre
historicEvents.description: King Aethelred ordered all Danish men in England to be killed. It is unknown how many Danes were killed due to this order.

historicEvents.title: Sweyn Forkbeard
historicEvents.description: The Danes raided and captured England. Sweyn, King of Danes, was declared King of England on Christmas Day.

historicEvents.title: Canut the Great
historicEvents.description: As Aethelred, King of England died, his son, Edmund Ironside devised a truce with Danish King Canute. The truce divided the kingdom between the two but Edmund died shortly after and became King of England. Several years later, he also became king of Denmark.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir was born in 0996, in Louviers, Eure, Normandy, France, his father, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, was 36 and his mother, Judith de Bayeux, was 34. He had at least 1 son with Adèle Osule. He died in 1038, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir est né en 0996, à Louviers, Eure, Normandie, France, son père, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Judith de Bayeux, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Adèle Osule. Il est décédé en 1038, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir 0996 in Louviers, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches 36 und seine Mutter Judith de Bayeux 34. Er hatte mit Adèle Osule mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1038 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir è nato il nel 0996 nel Louviers, Eure, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Judith de Bayeux, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Adèle Osule. Morì nel 1038, nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Tosny I of Belvoirが0996年にフランスノーマンディエウアロウヴィエルズで生まれた時，父Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conchesは36歳，母Judith de Bayeuxは34歳であった。彼はAdèle Osuleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1038年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir(이)가 0996년 프랑스 노먼디 우르 루비어스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith de Bayeux(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Adèle Osule(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1038년 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir nasceu em 0996, em Louviers, Eure, Normandie, França, seu pai, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Judith de Bayeux, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Adèle Osule. Ele faleceu em 1038, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir родился в 0996 г. в местности лоувиирс, эур, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith de Bayeux, 34. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Adèle Osule. Он умер в 1038 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir nació en 0996, en Louviers, Eure, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Judith de Bayeux, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Adèle Osule. Murió en 1038, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años.
